Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Consensus Board Game 31 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. The Shape of Inequalities 20 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Afroman Wins Civil Trial over Use of Police Raid Footage in His Music Videos 269 upvotes, 41 comments
  4. macOS 26 breaks custom DNS settings including .internal 48 upvotes, 5 comments
  5. Autoresearch for SAT Solvers 153 upvotes, 30 comments
  6. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 324 upvotes, 78 comments
  7. Nvidia greenboost: transparently extend GPU VRAM using system RAM/NVMe 431 upvotes, 119 comments
  8. Conway's Game of Life, in real life 263 upvotes, 69 comments
  9. Pretraining Language Models via Neural Cellular Automata 55 upvotes, 11 comments
  10. Gluon: Explicit Performance 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. OpenBSD: PF queues break the 4 Gbps barrier 72 upvotes, 20 comments
  12. How many branches can your CPU predict? 61 upvotes, 17 comments
  13. Show HN: Duplicate 3 layers in a 24B LLM, logical deduction .22→.76. No training 198 upvotes, 72 comments
  14. Afroman found not liable in defamation case 725 upvotes, 311 comments
  15. A Preview of Coalton 0.2 15 upvotes, 2 comments
  16. Juggalo Makeup Blocks Facial Recognition Technology (2019) 112 upvotes, 45 comments
  17. A sufficiently detailed spec is code 509 upvotes, 273 comments
  18. Eniac, the First General-Purpose Digital Computer, Turns 80 77 upvotes, 34 comments
  19. LotusNotes 151 upvotes, 76 comments
  20. Warranty Void If Regenerated 461 upvotes, 281 comments
  21. The math that explains why bell curves are everywhere 182 upvotes, 110 comments
  22. Oil and gas prices jump after Iran and Israel attack gasfields 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. 'Your Frustration Is the Product' 211 upvotes, 131 comments
  24. Nvidia NemoClaw 354 upvotes, 233 comments
  25. Astral to Join OpenAI 549 upvotes, 374 comments
  26. The next fight over the use of facial recognition could be in the supermarkets 35 upvotes, 15 comments
  27. Stdwin: Standard window interface by Guido Van Rossum [pdf] 64 upvotes, 37 comments
  28. 2% of ICML papers desk rejected because the authors used LLM in their reviews 156 upvotes, 132 comments
  29. Austin’s surge of new housing construction drove down rents 680 upvotes, 820 comments
  30. Hyper-optimized reverse geocoding API 7 upvotes, 4 comments
